warning: Proof history can be reduced to a Greek-European story.
mitigation: Include Mesopotamian, Egyptian, Indian, Chinese, Islamic, and other mathematical traditions.
responsible_history_note: proof history should recognize distinct traditions without reducing them to a single triumphalist path

warning: Past mathematical practices can be judged only by modern formal standards.
mitigation: Interpret proof practices within their own media, institutions, languages, and purposes.
responsible_history_note: proof history should recognize distinct traditions without reducing them to a single triumphalist path

warning: Different forms of justification can be treated as identical.
mitigation: Compare traditions carefully while preserving real differences in style and authority.
responsible_history_note: proof history should recognize distinct traditions without reducing them to a single triumphalist path

warning: Famous texts and surviving manuscripts can be mistaken for the whole history.
mitigation: Attend to pedagogy, commentary, craft, oral transmission, and institutional power.
responsible_history_note: proof history should recognize distinct traditions without reducing them to a single triumphalist path

warning: Machine-checked proof can be treated as the end of human proof.
mitigation: Recognize human choices in formalization, theorem selection, interpretation, and tool trust.
responsible_history_note: proof history should recognize distinct traditions without reducing them to a single triumphalist path

warning: Translation can erase terms, genres, and local meanings of justification.
mitigation: Preserve historical terminology and cite specialist scholarship when possible.
responsible_history_note: proof history should recognize distinct traditions without reducing them to a single triumphalist path
